title: 1 question please newbie here
wikitext:
[]: 1 question please - newbie here             Quote Post by jacobmm » Sun Feb 24, 2008 10:53 am Hello people. I havent seen this anime yet. Im waiting for my exams to be over to watch it-which is about 2 months from now. Just one question - is it necessary to watch 'Rebirth'? the one that comes after 'death' i suppose, because i read that its incomplete or so and that it dosent have any important role in the storyline. Is it better to not watch it at all? If i dont then would i be missing something good? help please! Ive seen from a few websites that this is the best anime there is. I certainly hope that i would love it Also tell me, im almost 18, am i too old/young for this anime/any anime? Im sorry i dont know if this is where im supposed to post this. I dont even know if im supposed to post here XD. Thanking you, Jacob M. Mathew

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Feb 24, 2008 11:13 am you don't need to watch Death and Rebirth at all: the first section is just a clip show summarizing the series, and the second section is the first half of "End of Evangelion". Just watch the normal series, then "End of Evangelion'.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Sun Feb 24, 2008 11:14 am What V said. Just watch the normal series and End of Evangelion. Edit: And for age, it depends on how sensitive you are. Some scenes are particularly violent, especially in End of Evangelion, but if you're okay with them, you can watch the series. The TV series is much less nasty. And do say hello in the welcome thread in the OT forum.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sun Feb 24, 2008 11:19 am Death : spliced footage from the TV series. Rebirth : first half hour of End of Evangelion. There's no reason to watch Rebirth if you have End of Evangelion. End of Evangelion is rated 15+.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Feb 24, 2008 2:21 pm Oh...15+? My bad...lol. I was 12 when I first started watching the series, 13 when I saw the movie. As long as you're ok with seeing violence, nudity (not so much), and things that might fuck with the way you think. Then you should be good. As for Death and Rebirth. It was pretty much just a recap of the series, but with some extra footage if you can spot it.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Feb 24, 2008 10:06 pm I find it funny EoE is rated 15+. Over here, it's like insta-R, which is 17+. I dunno, I guess I just wonder how bad something needs to be to get rated higher than 15+... RE OP: V got it right about D&R. The series and film I'd surmise are fine for ages 15+, assuming you can stand the violence and emotional impact. There's no such thing as being "too old" for the series. I introduced a 55 year old cinephile to it and he instantly fell in love with it.

[]:             Quote Post by TheAyanamiOtaku » Mon Feb 25, 2008 1:32 am don't listen to anyone, watch everything. it's worth it man. everything anno makes is a different prespective on the Evangelion timeline. so watch everything.

[]:             Quote Post by Shin-seiki » Mon Feb 25, 2008 5:13 pm TheAyanamiOtaku wrote: don't listen to anyone, watch everything. it's worth it man. everything anno makes is a different prespective on the Evangelion timeline. so watch everything.

[]:             Quote Post by jacobmm » Mon Feb 25, 2008 5:42 pm Thank you all so much for your replies. Please correct me if im wrong: 1) I need not watch 'Rebirth' because it is "exactly" the same as the start of EoE? 2)'Death' is that one which ends saying 'intermission' with a countdown. The trouble was that i downloaded the series and the movies. The series were dubbed in english while the movies were Japanese subtitled in english- except for 'Rebirth' which was not subtitled and was in a forein language(french i think). Thanks again- gotta get some sleep now.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Feb 25, 2008 8:15 pm jacobmm wrote: : 1) I need not watch 'Rebirth' because it is "exactly" the same as the start of EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Feb 25, 2008 8:19 pm UrsusArctos wrote: jacobmm wrote: : 1) I need not watch 'Rebirth' because it is "exactly" the same as the start of EoE?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Feb 26, 2008 2:49 pm Everything in the series is good for children of all ages. Who it's bad for is their parents.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Feb 26, 2008 4:03 pm Or people who is already mentally unstable, as NGE would make it worse. I have seen it happen. Tragic.

[]:             Quote Post by shito » Tue Feb 26, 2008 6:11 pm nice job with the comparisions there... I think if you're not in a hurry is just better to watch Death & Rebirth too. I didn't knew the Manga release of Rebirth was just taken from Air, how sucky haha. Also I think the way Rebirth ends and the ending theme is something worth watching (although I can say it's frustrating if you don't have The End to watch right after it)

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Tue Feb 26, 2008 6:36 pm The Imperialist wrote: Or people who is already mentally unstable, as NGE would make it worse. I have seen it happen. Tragic.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Feb 26, 2008 7:38 pm I think they would go into a really depressed by the time they reach the end.

[]:             Quote Post by Nigoki » Sat Mar 01, 2008 11:45 pm There's no reason to watch Rebirth if you have End of Evangelion. End of Evangelion is rated 15+.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Thu Mar 06, 2008 1:59 am The Imperialist wrote: I think they would go into a really depressed by the time they reach the end.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Thu Mar 06, 2008 6:03 pm Jimbo wrote: <snippy> I think the series is emotionally unflinching and psychotic enough to have any kind of reaction to.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Mar 06, 2008 6:27 pm Some paraphrased quote by somebody or other wrote: Evangelion only makes sense if you're already depressed. Normal people don't get it.
